engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi i Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 0.66L Petrol
entries.history: Naturally aspirated 3-cylinder kei engine delivering efficient urban mobility with minimal turbo complexity for budget-conscious owners.
commonFailures: Oil seepage around spark plug wells and ignition coils (typical 40k-70k miles)
Valve stem seal wear causing blue smoke on cold start
Cooling system leaks due to rear-engine heat concentration

commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference design - catastrophic valve/piston contact if snapped)

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi i Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi i's compact 660cc 3-cylinder engines are generally robust, but the rear-mounted layout creates unique cooling challenges and the 3B20 turbo's interference design makes timing belt maintenance critical. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Mitsubishi i 3B20 Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi i 660cc Turbo, Turbo MIVEC, Turbo 4WD (2006-2013, 3B20 & 3B20M)
problems.rootCause: The 3B20 is an interference engine; if the timing belt snaps or jumps teeth, valves contact pistons causing catastrophic internal damage requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Belt Replacement - Before failure occurs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idler pulleys and water pump
repairOptions.longevity: Restores reliability; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.tier: Catastrophic Failure Repair - After belt snaps
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head repair, valve replacement, piston inspection, belt system rebuild
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if damage is minor; often not cost-effective

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Post-failure solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned 3B20 with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; avoids hidden damage risks

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi i worth £2,500-£4,500, preventative timing belt replacement at 60k miles is far more economical than risking catastrophic failure. If the belt has already snapped, a rebuilt engine with warranty offers better value than attempting a partial repair.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 3B20 engine prices for your Mitsubishi i

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Common Issue Across Mitsubishi i 3A20 and 3B20 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi i 660cc Base, Turbo, all variants (2006-2013, 3A20 & 3B20)
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings, valve stem seals, or turbo oil seals allow oil to enter combustion chambers or leak externally, exacerbated by rear-engine heat concentration.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Seal Replacement - Early-stage oil seepage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve stem seals, spark plug well seals, turbo oil feed lines
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for mild consumption; may not address piston ring wear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Top-End Rebuild - Moderate oil burn
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, valve guide/seal replacement, decarbonising
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses valve-related consumption; piston rings remain original

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Severe consumption
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned short/long block with upgraded rings and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Most comprehensive solution; eliminates all internal wear sources

problems.recommendation: If your Mitsubishi i is consuming over 1 litre per 1,000 miles, a rebuilt engine often provides better long-term value than repeated seal replacements, especially given the rear-engine access complexity.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi i engine replacement addressing oil consumption

problems.h4: Cooling System Failure - Rear-Engine Heat Management Challenges
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-85,000 miles, accelerated by short-trip urban use
problems.rootCause: The boot-mounted engine layout restricts airflow and complicates coolant routing; radiator, hoses and water pump failures lead to overheating and potential head gasket damage.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Component Replacement - Single part failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator, thermostat, hoses or water pump individually
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated failures; inspect full system for wear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Cooling System Overhaul - Multiple aged components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace radiator, hoses, thermostat, water pump and flush system
repairOptions.longevity: Restores cooling reliability; recommended for vehicles over 70k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Post-overheating damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned engine with new head gasket, pressure tested
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if overheating caused warping or head gasket failure

problems.recommendation: Given the Mitsubishi i's rear-engine cooling challenges, a full cooling system overhaul at 70k miles is a wise preventative investment. If overheating has already occurred, inspect for head gasket integrity before committing to repairs.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi i cooling system specialists near you

problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi i 660cc Turbo, Turbo MIVEC, Turbo 4WD (2006-2013, 3B20 & 3B20M)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 55,000-90,000 miles depending on oil change frequency
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed line coking, worn compressor bearings, or wastegate actuator failure cause boost loss, oil consumption or excessive smoke.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Service - Early warning signs
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean oil feed lines, replace actuator, check wastegate operation
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for mild boost loss or intermittent issues

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement - Confirmed turbo failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned turbocharger unit with new gaskets and lines
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance; ensure oil system is clean to protect new unit

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Turbo damage to engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned 3B20 with upgraded turbo, full system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil starvation or debris ingress

problems.recommendation: Regular oil changes every 5,000 miles are critical for 3B20 turbo longevity. If boost pressure is inconsistent or blue smoke appears, address turbo issues promptly to avoid secondary engine damage.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi i 3B20 turbo engine replacement prices

problems.h4: Ignition System Oil Seepage - 3A20 Spark Plug Well Weakness
problems.rootCause: Valve cover gasket degradation allows oil to pool in spark plug wells, causing misfires, coil pack failure and difficult starting.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Seal Replacement - Early oil seepage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve cover gasket, clean spark plug wells, inspect coils
repairOptions.longevity: Highly effective for early-stage seepage; prevents coil damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Coil Replacement + Seals - Misfire present
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace valve cover gasket plus failed ignition coils and spark plugs
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptoms; ensure all coils are inspected for oil contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Severe neglect damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit reconditioned 3A20 if oil ingress caused internal corrosion
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed; seal replacement usually resolves issue completely

problems.recommendation: The 3A20 spark plug well seepage is easily and affordably fixed with a valve cover gasket replacement. Address it at first sign of misfire to avoid costly coil pack replacements.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi i 3A20 ignition system repair

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi i
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi i's unique rear-engine kei design demands specialist knowledge for reliable repairs. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand 3A20 and 3B20 quirks, offering rebuilt engines with upgraded components that address original failure points. Compare prices from vetted suppliers, all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi i Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi i and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.descriptor: The Mitsubishi i exclusively uses 0.66L three-cylinder petrol engines, either naturally aspirated (3A20) or turbocharged MIVEC (3B20/3B20M), all complying with Japanese kei car regulations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi i Petrol Engine Families
families: 3A20 - Naturally aspirated base engine (54hp), prone to spark plug well oil seepage
3B20 - Turbocharged performance unit (64hp), timing belt critical due to interference design
3B20M - MIVEC variable valve timing turbo, refined throttle response for later models
foundIn: First generation (2006-2011) - 3A20 NA and early 3B20 turbo
Facelift generation (2012-2013) - Updated 3B20M MIVEC turbo with refined mapping
knownFor: Exceptional fuel economy and low road tax due to kei car classification
Rear-engine layout creates unique cooling and access challenges for mechanics
Timing belt maintenance critical on turbo models to avoid catastrophic valve damage
Oil consumption issues common across both NA and turbo variants at higher mileage

engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi i's 2006-2013 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: 3A20 NA and 3B20 Turbo Debut
years.description: The Mitsubishi i launched with two 660cc three-cylinder options: the economical 3A20 naturally aspirated and the performance-focused 3B20 turbo, both rear-mounted.
keyChanges: Mitsubishi i kei car launched in UK market with rear-engine, rear-wheel-drive layout
Two engine options introduced: 54hp 3A20 NA and 64hp 3B20 turbo MIVEC
Unique boot-floor engine mounting requiring specialist removal procedures
knownFor: Compact dimensions ideal for urban parking and narrow streets
Rear-engine layout provides excellent traction but complicates cooling
Early models show oil seepage at spark plug wells on 3A20 units
Timing belt replacement critical on 3B20 to avoid interference damage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006 Mitsubishi i replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi i 3B20 timing belt replacement • Mitsubishi i 3A20 oil consumption fix • Mitsubishi i rear engine removal cost
enquiries: Mitsubishi i rear engine removal cost

years.description: MIVEC variable valve timing added to the 3B20 turbo for smoother power delivery, while 4WD variants gained popularity among UK kei enthusiasts.
keyChanges: MIVEC technology introduced on 3B20 turbo (designated 3B20M) for improved throttle response
4WD variants expanded across both NA and turbo engine options
Minor interior updates and improved sound insulation for cabin refinement
mainEngines: 3B20M - 0.66L petrol turbo MIVEC (Turbo MIVEC, Turbo MIVEC 4WD)
knownFor: MIVEC turbo delivers exceptionally smooth power for a 660cc engine with minimal lag
4WD system adds weight but improves all-weather capability for UK roads
Cooling system upgrades implemented to address early overheating concerns
Oil consumption remains a monitored issue across both engine codes at higher mileage
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008 Mitsubishi i replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi i MIVEC turbo replacement • Mitsubishi i 4WD engine compatibility • Mitsubishi i cooling system overhaul

years.description: The facelifted Mitsubishi i received subtle styling updates and refined engine management for the final production years, with the 3B20M MIVEC turbo as the sole performance option.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift with updated bumpers, lights and interior trim refinements
Engine management software updated for improved emissions and fuel economy
Final production run before Mitsubishi i discontinuation in 2013
mainEngines: 3B20M - 0.66L petrol turbo MIVEC (Facelift Turbo, Facelift Turbo MIVEC)
knownFor: Most refined iteration of the Mitsubishi i with improved NVH and throttle mapping
Later models benefit from updated cooling components addressing early weaknesses
High collector interest for low-mileage examples as kei car enthusiasts seek final-year units
Timing belt maintenance remains critical; interference design unchanged
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012 Mitsubishi i replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi i facelift turbo engine • Mitsubishi i final year engine availability • Mitsubishi i collector model engine sourcing
enquiries: Mitsubishi i final year engine availability

faq.h2: Mitsubishi i Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi i engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi i engine typically costs £550-£720 supply-only, with supply & fit adding £350-£550 labour depending on specialist location and rear-engine access complexity.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 3A20 naturally aspirated engines range £550-£675
Rebuilt 3B20 turbo engines range £585-£720 due to turbo components
Supply & fit labour adds £350-£550 for rear-engine specialist work
All EngineMarket suppliers include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get a tailored Mitsubishi i engine replacement quote today

items.question: What is the difference between Mitsubishi i 3A20 and 3B20 engines?
items.answer: The 3A20 is naturally aspirated (54hp) while the 3B20 is turbocharged MIVEC (64hp); they are not interchangeable without significant modification due to different mounting and ECU requirements.
keyPoints: 3A20: 54hp, no turbo, simpler maintenance, prone to spark plug well oil seepage
3B20: 64hp, turbo MIVEC, performance focus, timing belt critical (interference engine)
Engine codes must match exactly when replacing; 3A20 cannot substitute for 3B20
Turbo models command higher replacement prices due to forced induction components
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi i 3A20 vs 3B20 engine prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mitsubishi i engine or should I buy a different car?
items.answer: If your Mitsubishi i is otherwise in good condition with low mileage, a rebuilt engine at £550-£720 plus fitting is often more economical than purchasing another kei car in today's market.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost is typically 30-50% of a comparable used Mitsubishi i purchase price
Kei car values have risen; replacement often extends life of a known, maintained vehicle
Factor in rear-engine specialist labour when budgeting for total replacement cost
EngineMarket warranty protects your investment for 12 months minimum
items.cta: Find out if Mitsubishi i engine replacement makes financial sense for you

items.question: How long does it take to fit a Mitsubishi i engine?
items.answer: Due to the unique rear-engine, boot-floor mounting, expect 1-2 days for specialist removal and refitting, longer if cooling system or ancillary components require attention.
keyPoints: Rear-engine access requires boot floor removal and specialist equipment
Typical labour time: 8-16 hours depending on variant and condition
Allow extra time for cooling system flush and ECU relearn procedures
Book with a garage experienced in kei car or rear-engine layouts
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi i engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi i engines to fail most commonly?
items.answer: Timing belt failure on turbo 3B20 engines and oil seepage at spark plug wells on 3A20 units are the most frequent failure patterns driving replacement enquiries.
keyPoints: 3B20: Timing belt snapped = catastrophic valve/piston contact (interference design)
3A20: Valve cover gasket degradation allows oil into spark plug wells causing misfires
Cooling system failures affect both codes due to rear-engine heat concentration
Regular maintenance at 5,000-mile intervals significantly extends engine life
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi i engine replacement options addressing common failures

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi i engine myself to save money?
items.answer: Due to the rear-engine, boot-floor mounting and kei-car specific torque specifications, DIY fitting is not recommended unless you have specialist equipment and rear-engine experience.
keyPoints: Boot floor removal requires specific tools and procedures to avoid damage
Rear-engine cooling routing and ECU connections need precise reconnection
Incorrect torque on rear engine mounts can cause vibration or premature failure
Supply & fit from a specialist often proves more economical than DIY errors
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi i engine supply & fit from vetted specialists

items.question: Does a rebuilt Mitsubishi i engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt Mitsubishi i engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects in rebuilt components
Must be fitted by an approved specialist to maintain warranty validity
Keep all service records and compression test reports for warranty claims
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options at additional cost
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi i rebuilt engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Mitsubishi i has?
items.answer: Check your V5C registration document, the engine bay identification plate, or use EngineMarket's DVLA-linked registration lookup to instantly verify your exact 3A20 or 3B20 code.
keyPoints: Engine code stamped on block near gearbox interface (visible with boot floor removed)
V5C document section V.5 may list engine code for later registrations
DVLA lookup via registration number provides instant variant and engine confirmation
Never guess engine code; 3A20 and 3B20 are not directly interchangeable
items.cta: Verify your Mitsubishi i engine code instantly with our registration tool

items.question: Are Mitsubishi i engines reliable long-term?
items.answer: With timely timing belt changes on turbo models and attention to cooling system maintenance, both 3A20 and 3B20 engines can reliably exceed 100,000 miles in UK conditions.
keyPoints: 3A20 naturally aspirated units are mechanically simple and robust if oil leaks addressed
3B20 turbo requires strict 60k-mile timing belt changes to avoid catastrophic failure
Rear-engine layout demands vigilant cooling system inspection and fluid changes
Regular 5,000-mile oil changes critical due to high-revving kei engine design
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt Mitsubishi i engines with upgraded components

Specialists understand kei-car specific torque specs and cooling routing
EngineMarket vets all suppliers for Mitsubishi i expertise and warranty compliance
Nationwide delivery of engines plus local fitting options available
